title: Content Delivery Network Cache Hit Ratio
app: CDN raw logs / analytics API via HEC
spl:
index=telecom sourcetype="cdn:performance"
| eval hit=if(lower(cache_status) IN ("hit","tcp_hit","mem_hit"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(request_count) as reqs, sum(eval(if(hit=1,request_count,0))) as hit_reqs by pop_id, _time
| eval hit_ratio=round(100*hit_reqs/nullif(reqs,0),2)
| where hit_ratio < 85 AND reqs > 1000
| table _time, pop_id, reqs, hit_reqs, hit_ratio
description: Low cache hit ratios increase origin load and subscriber latency; trending hit ratio by POP and content type guides cache sizing and TTL policy without analyzing subscriber CDRs.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Content Delivery Network Cache Hit Ratio to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Map vendor cache hit tokens to `cache_status` in props. Exclude purge and error responses from denominator if tagged. Compare POPs to identify misconfigured origins. **Domain context:** CDN hit ratio varies by content type (live vs static) and TTL policy‚Äîcompare POPs with similar traffic mix. **Splunk:** Extend `hit`/`cache_status` mapping for Fastly/Akamai/CloudFront variants (`MISS`, `STALE`, etc.); `reqs > 1000` avoids noise on low-traffic POPs.
visualization: Line chart (hit ratio by POP), Bar chart (worst POPs), Map (if geo coordinates available).
knownFalsePositives: Cache hit ratio dips during purges, new origin deployments, DDoS or flash crowds, or TLS session ticket rotation the CDN TAM confirmed as an expected pattern.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: CDN raw logs / analytics API via HEC.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=telecom` `sourcetype="cdn:performance"` (pop_id, cache_status, bytes_served, request_count).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Map vendor cache hit tokens to `cache_status` in props. Exclude purge and error responses from denominator if tagged. Compare POPs to identify misconfigured origins. **Domain context:** CDN hit ratio varies by content type (live vs static) and TTL policy‚Äîcompare POPs with similar traffic mix. **Splunk:** Extend `hit`/`cache_status` mapping for Fastly/Akamai/CloudFront variants (`MISS`, `STALE`, etc.); `reqs > 1000` avoids noise on low-traffic POPs.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=telecom sourcetype="cdn:performance"
| eval hit=if(lower(cache_status) IN ("hit","tcp_hit","mem_hit"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(request_count) as reqs, sum(eval(if(hit=1,request_count,0))) as hit_reqs by pop_id, _time
| eval hit_ratio=round(100*hit_reqs/nullif(reqs,0),2)
| where hit_ratio < 85 AND reqs > 1000
| table _time, pop_id, reqs, hit_reqs, hit_ratio
```

Understanding this SPL

**Content Delivery Network Cache Hit Ratio** ‚Äî Low cache hit ratios increase origin load and subscriber latency; trending hit ratio by POP and content type guides cache sizing and TTL policy without analyzing subscriber CDRs.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=telecom` `sourcetype="cdn:performance"` (pop_id, cache_status, bytes_served, request_count). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): CDN raw logs / analytics API via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: telecom; **sourcetype**: cdn:performance. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=telecom, sourcetype="cdn:performance". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **hit** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pop_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **hit_ratio** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where hit_ratio < 85 AND reqs > 1000` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Content Delivery Network Cache Hit Ratio**): table _time, pop_id, reqs, hit_reqs, hit_ratio


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare KPIs or record counts with the EMS/performance management export or mediation control room view for the same element and interval. Align vendor time zones and collection periods before tuning thresholds.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (hit ratio by POP), Bar chart (worst POPs), Map (if geo coordinates available).
grandmaExplanation: We watch CDN hit ratios and origin load. We help you catch bad caching before video stalls spike.
